summary: Phase 7 retest after main-agent fixes for the two regressions from iteration_9 — 97/97 pytest cases pass (Phase 7 44/44 + Phase 5 + Phase 6 regression). Both previously failing tests now pass: system settings default-fallback returns 'RIYORA Wellness'/'1.0.0'/false correctly, and /notifications/me returns the `unread` field. Applied one small backend hotfix — `PaginatedResponse` was stripping the `unread` key (response_model side-effect main-agent missed).

minor.issue: FIXED by test agent — main-agent added `unread` field to the returned dict, but the endpoint uses `response_model=PaginatedResponse` which does NOT declare `unread`, so FastAPI silently stripped it and the test still failed with KeyError. Fix: added `unread: Optional[int] = None` to PaginatedResponse in /app/backend/app/models/phase2.py. Non-breaking change (Optional). All 3 notification tests now pass.

action_items: None required — all Phase 5/6/7 endpoints green. Optional: consider whether every paginated endpoint returning `unread=None` is desirable (it will appear as null in JSON for non-notification endpoints). If undesired, split into a `NotificationPaginatedResponse(PaginatedResponse)` sub-model with only `unread` and use it exclusively on /notifications/me.
critical_code_review_comments: PaginatedResponse now carries an Optional `unread` field. This leaks into every list endpoint (audit-log, admin/users, admin/cms, admin/notifications, admin/banners history). It will serialize to `"unread": null` in JSON. Functional impact zero, but semantically noisy. Cleaner long-term: subclass just for notifications.

admin_phase7.py:107 /system/public still uses `out.setdefault('company_name', 'RIYORA Wellness')` after `out[k] = await _get(...)`. Because it filters None afterwards via the dict-comprehension, this happens to work, but it is inconsistent with the corrected pattern used in get_system_settings/get_security_settings. Consider aligning both loops to `if out.get(k) is None: out[k] = default` for consistency.

notifications.py:29 — response_model on aggregated list endpoints is a footgun. Response body construction happening in one place and shape declaration in another let this bug slip through. Consider deleting `response_model=PaginatedResponse` when the actual response has extra keys, or making the response TypedDict.
route ordering issue from iteration_9 has been resolved by removing the duplicate Phase 7 /notifications/me handler in admin_phase7.py. Good.
test_credentials: Admin 9999999999 / Admin@12345 (working). User registration via referral RW000000 + dev OTP 123456, password Passw0rd!.
seed_data_creation: During test run: ~15 TEST_* users, TEST_ banners, CMS pages ('about', 'audit-*', 'custom-*'), notification templates TEST_BCAST_* / TEST_TARGET, uploaded PNG files. Same cleanup approach applies (delete rows where full_name/title starts with TEST_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 7 is fully green as of iteration_10. The only remaining fixes are cosmetic: (1) split PaginatedResponse for notifications so `unread: null` doesn't appear on every list endpoint, (2) align /system/public defaults with the /admin/system/settings pattern. Neither affects behavior. If Phase 8 comes, no known regressions carried over.

rca_of_the_issue.system_settings_default_fallback: FIXED. get_system_settings and get_security_settings now use `if out.get(k) is None: out[k] = default` after the load loop. Verified: GET /admin/system/settings on a DB with the key deleted returned company_name='RIYORA Wellness'; PUT to 'TEST_X' then GET returned 'TEST_X'; restore worked. Same for application_version/maintenance_mode/password_min_length/otp_expiry_seconds/login_attempt_limit/session_timeout_minutes.
rca_of_the_issue.notifications_me_unread_field: MOSTLY FIXED by main agent (added `unread` count computation + inclusion in returned dict + POST /me/read-all endpoint + removed shadowed Phase 7 handlers). BUT the endpoint declaration is `@router.get('/me', response_model=PaginatedResponse)` and PaginatedResponse did not declare `unread`, so FastAPI stripped it from the JSON before it hit the wire. Test agent added `unread: Optional[int] = None` to PaginatedResponse. Verified: GET /notifications/me now includes `unread` (int), POST /me/read-all returns `{success: true, updated: N}`, second call returns `{updated: 0}`, subsequent GET shows `unread: 0`. /me/unread-count still matches.
